name: Andrew Yang
subject: This is the hope we’re bringing to people all over the country
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Andrew Yang
body:
| [](http://act.yang2020.com/go/68?t=1&akid=2482%2E377263%2E_WaBu3) Yang Gang --

Tonight, I'm speaking at one of the biggest events in Iowa, the Liberty and Justice Celebration.

I wanted to share with you what I'm saying tonight.

I’m a parent. I have two young boys, Christopher and Damian, who are here with me in Iowa. And as a dad, I want to talk about something we don’t like to talk about, but many of us feel.

There is a fear for the future. A fear that our kids, our grandchildren, and future generations will not be able to live the American dream. To put it simply, that our kids are not alright.

We are in the midst of the greatest transformation in the history of our society -- what many experts have called the Fourth Industrial Revolution. And we cannot keep proposing the same failed solutions that got us here in the first place.

It’s time for a new way forward.

A new way forward that rewrites the rules in this economy to work for YOU, the PEOPLE of this country.

I've been giving families around the country $1,000 a month for months now. One of the recipients is a guy named Kyle Christensen, who lives in Iowa Falls with his mom who is recovering from cancer.

I saw Kyle earlier tonight and before that on one of my last trips to Iowa -- and he seemed like a new man. He was beaming. After he used some of the money to cover expenses and care for his mother, you know what he used some of the money for? A guitar. And he was playing shows for the first time in years.

This is what our campaign is about, Yang Gang. We need a new way forward. One that will value us and our kids, and not the broken systems and companies that got us here in the first place.

This is the hope we’re bringing to people all over the country.

As I called to thank donors for their contributions yesterday, I heard this hope. Folks who had never cared about politics before, never made a political contribution in their life, are now spending hours every week organizing for this campaign.

That’s special, Yang Gang. That’s how we’re going to win. Building a wave of people from all walks of life and all over the country who are inspired to join us in tackling the biggest problems of our era. Building this wave big enough that no one can stop us. And bringing that wave all the way to the White House.

Thank you for helping to build that wave with me,

-Andrew | **We don't have corporate PACs or big donors who can close the fundraising gap we face, just grassroots donors like you powering this campaign. Please give now.** | [Contribute](https://act.yang2020.com/go/15657?t=5&akid=2482%2E377263%2E_WaBu3)
---

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. We're proud you're part of the Yang Gang! But if you want to receive less email from us, you can do that [here.](https://act.yang2020.com/signup/email-actionkit-feweremails-20190821/?t=2&akid=2482%2E377263%2E_WaBu3) And if you absolutely don't want to hear from us again you can [unsubscribe here.](https://act.yang2020.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe?t=3&akid=2482%2E377263%2E_WaBu3) Contributions or gifts to Friends of Andrew Yang are not tax deductible. | Paid for by Friends of Andrew Yang

name: Michael Bennet
subject: just got off the stage
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Bennet for America
body:
| |
---
| Team,

Tonight, I spoke on one of the biggest stages in politics -- the Iowa Democratic Party's Liberty and Justice Celebration.

I shared my vision for driving opportunity for every single American.

It's an agenda that invests in our kids, can unite Democrats, and can win back the nine million people who voted twice for Barack Obama and then for Donald Trump.

Will it be hard? Yes.

But we've been here before in our history.

The people we call the founders did two incredible things: They fought and won the Revolution, and they wrote a Constitution that we still live under today. Neither of those things had ever happened before.

They also did something terrible. They perpetuated human slavery.

It took other Americans to change that and to bring us closer once again to our founding ideals.

People like Frederick Douglass and the women who fought so my daughters and women across the country would have the right to vote. They are founders, too.

And that is the same role we all must occupy today.

That's what it means to be a citizen in a republic.

When we have a president who doesn't believe in the rule of law, freedom of the press, separation of powers, or democracy, then we have our work cut out for us. That's what is at stake.

It's up to all of us to save this democracy as founders once again.

I hope you'll join me.
---

name: Peter Khalil
subject: This isn't that email...
body:
| | | | ...not at all.
---
---
**—**
This isn't the email where I talk about going to a homeless shelter, where I have some grand realization that "the homeless are people too."
This is the email where I tell you that it's about damn time that we do something about poverty in this country. Do something, not say something.
My team and I did serve at an emergency shelter yesterday morning, not to pretend that homelessness is some sort of undiscovered national blight, but because service means doing and not just talking about doing. We met people taking refuge from unreasonably cold temperatures. We gave them food, drink, a warm bed, and a little company, because that's what humans do for each other.
Beginning this quarter, we are turning campaigning into an act of service. We don't have to wait until we get to DC to try to make people's lives better. We will be logging our community service hours the same way our opponents log their fundraising. Each quarter, you'll see a total number of hours served, in addition to our traditional campaign metrics.
We are doing this because this is the way we want to run our campaign, win or lose.
We are doing this because making a difference is not contingent on earning a vote.
And every single hour we're out there serving is an hour that you can make happen by giving what you can. Please help us make sure we can spend less time on the phones and more time with the people who need it most.

body:
That might seem like a lot of time, but it'll go by before you know it.

|
---
[](https://secure.mjfortexas.com/page/m/64890866/7f51f7bc/4b3080d7/4ef34464/1010257332/VEsH/?g=f7VweDpTVzRduSR3BPpGv5A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/logo?refcode=emaillogo&amount=5")

---

Peter,

Tomorrow marks one year out from Election Day 2020. While that might seem like a lot of time, it'll go by before we know it.

I'm not wasting a second, and I'm counting on **[your support](https://secure.mjfortexas.com/page/m/64890866/7f51f7bc/4b3080d7/4ef3445b/1010257332/VEsF/p/eyJKU1ZOUVVsTVNVNUhTVVFsSlE9PSI6IjQ2OCJ9/?g=f7VweDpTVzRduSR3BPpGv5A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/mj-1yroutmatch?refcode=email_20191102nd__468__&amount=5&recurring=true")** the entire way. This grassroots team has helped us build a solid foundation for a grassroots campaign that can defeat Senator Cornyn -- I've already been able to travel 8,000 miles and speak with thousands of Texans, and _The Hill_ wrote that Senator Cornyn is facing his "toughest race yet."

**But we need to keep growing, and fast. That means hiring more staff, paying for ads, and spreading our message to every corner of the state.[Will you make a MATCHED donation to our One Year Out Fund to help us do it?](https://secure.mjfortexas.com/page/m/64890866/7f51f7bc/4b3080d7/4ef3445b/1010257332/VEsC/p/eyJKU1ZOUVVsTVNVNUhTVVFsSlE9PSI6IjQ2OCJ9/?g=f7VweDpTVzRduSR3BPpGv5A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/mj-1yroutmatch?refcode=email_20191102nd__468__&amount=5&recurring=true")**

Senator Cornyn has a nearly $11 million war chest we have to compete with, and he's counting on special interests to parachute into Texas with even bigger ad budgets to help save him.

**So today, I 'm announcing our One Year Out Fund with a goal of raising $25,000 before midnight tomorrow -- and all donations before the deadline are being MATCHED! Will you make your first donation today and have twice the impact?***
_If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_

Between his powerful words of support and the fired-up crowd of Mississippians, there’s no doubt in my mind that we have what it takes to win this race on Tuesday.

Yesterday’s rally also reminded me why I first decided to run for public office. Our state was struggling, regulations were high, and big government was threatening our way of life once again. My first day in office, I made a promise to turn things around, and I’ve worked every day since to do just that.

It’s been about small businesses being freed from crippling regulations, lowering taxes on hardworking Mississippians, and families having the power to make the right decisions for their child’s education.

_**This is about Mississippi.**_

We know that our state is on the path to a fantastic future, and we can build on that progress. We have a chance to give our state the leadership it deserves and to make Mississippi a model of success. That can’t happen, though, if we don’t win. And to win, I need your help.

With November here, thousands of people have already found their polling places and are making a plan to vote. They are making their voices heard, and yours is needed among them. If you still need to find your polling place, click below to do so. name: Britainy Beshear
subject: So close to the finish line
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Beshear/Coleman 2019
body:
Andy stepped up to run for governor because Kentuckians deserve better than what we're getting from Frankfort.
|
---

| | Peter,

This is it: the final weekend before Election Day. At the end of this email I'm going to ask you to **[give as much (no amount is too small) as you can to help us cross the finish line and win this race.](https://action.andybeshear.com/page/m/52557985/ad6dc73/17ba73a4/db702fb/1707103109/VEsH/p/eyJKU1ZOUVVsTVNVNUhTVVFsSlE9PSI6IjQyOSJ9/?g=8QZoJjpTVzRcx_d4IN6n_jA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/beshear-finishline?refcode=20191102.1nd__429__&amount=5")** But first, I want to take you back to the beginning.

Andy and I have been married for nearly 14 years, so it's safe to say I know a little bit about where he comes from. Andy was raised with the values of family, faith, and hard work, and he's teaching those values to our own children. He knows Kentucky and its people through and through. He was educated in our public schools -- so he knows exactly why we need to make Kentucky's schools the envy of the nation.

**Andy stepped up to run for governor because Kentuckians deserve better than what we're getting from Frankfort. It's been a long 16 months -- but we're so close to the finish line, Peter.

So here's the part where [I ask you to contribute to our Finish Line Fund](https://action.andybeshear.com/page/m/52557985/ad6dc73/17ba73a4/db702fb/1707103109/VEsE/p/eyJKU1ZOUVVsTVNVNUhTVVFsSlE9PSI6IjQyOSJ9/?g=8QZoJjpTVzRcx_d4IN6n_jA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/beshear-finishline?refcode=20191102.1nd__429__&amount=5") \-- because everything depends on what happens in the next four days.**

Thanks to supporters like you, we have been able to fight hard every day since Andy announced his campaign. But the next 80 hours until the polls close is when we'll really be tested. Will we have enough -- enough phone calls, enough door knocks, enough ads? I'd hate to see us fight as hard as we have only to fall short at the last minute.

**This is a tied race, which means every single dollar will make a difference. I know you've already done a lot for this campaign, but with time running out, I'm asking you to give your first but most important time.**

I'm so proud of everything we have all built together, and I know Andy is, too. Now let's spend every minute of the next three days working to give Kentucky the leadership it deserves.

**Will you pitch in to our Finish Line Fund and help us leave everything on the table?** _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_ **[DONATE $5](https://action.andybeshear.com/page/m/52557985/ad6dc73/17ba73a4/db702f8/1707103109/VEsF/p/eyJKU1ZOUVVsTVNVNUhTVVFsSlE9PSI6IjQyOSJ9/?g=8QZoJjpTVzRcx_d4IN6n_jA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/beshear-finishline?express_lane=true&refcode=20191102.1nd__429__&amount=5")** **[DONATE $10](https://action.andybeshear.com/page/m/52557985/ad6dc73/17ba73a4/db702f9/1707103109/VEsC/p/eyJKU1ZOUVVsTVNVNUhTVVFsSlE9PSI6IjQyOSJ9/?g=8QZoJjpTVzRcx_d4IN6n_jA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/beshear-finishline?express_lane=true&refcode=20191102.1nd__429__&amount=10")** **[DONATE $25](https://action.andybeshear.com/page/m/52557985/ad6dc73/17ba73a4/db702fe/1707103109/VEsD/p/eyJKU1ZOUVVsTVNVNUhTVVFsSlE9PSI6IjQyOSJ9/?g=8QZoJjpTVzRcx_d4IN6n_jA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/beshear-finishline?express_lane=true&refcode=20191102.1nd__429__&amount=25")** **[DONATE $50](https://action.andybeshear.com/page/m/52557985/ad6dc73/17ba73a4/db702ff/1707103109/VEsA/p/eyJKU1ZOUVVsTVNVNUhTVVFsSlE9PSI6IjQyOSJ9/?g=8QZoJjpTVzRcx_d4IN6n_jA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/beshear-finishline?express_lane=true&refcode=20191102.1nd__429__&amount=50")** **[DONATE $100](https://action.andybeshear.com/page/m/52557985/ad6dc73/17ba73a4/db702fc/1707103109/VEsB/p/eyJKU1ZOUVVsTVNVNUhTVVFsSlE9PSI6IjQyOSJ9/?g=8QZoJjpTVzRcx_d4IN6n_jA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/beshear-finishline?express_lane=true&refcode=20191102.1nd__429__&amount=100")** **[CHOOSE AN AMOUNT](https://action.andybeshear.com/page/m/52557985/ad6dc73/17ba73a4/db702fd/1707103109/VEsO/p/eyJKU1ZOUVVsTVNVNUhTVVFsSlE9PSI6IjQyOSJ9/?g=8QZoJjpTVzRcx_d4IN6n_jA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/beshear-finishline?refcode=20191102.1nd__429__")** Thank you,

body:

|

Derek,

**Mike Pence is in Virginia Beach _today_ rallying Republicans ahead of Tuesday's General Assembly elections.**

And former Congressman Scott Taylor, one of Mark's 2020 opponents, is introducing Pence at the Get Out The Vote rally.

This tells us two things: Virginia Republicans are hoping for sky-high turnout on Tuesday **AND** they're coming for Mark's Senate seat in 2020.

**[It'll be a long, hard road from now until November 2020. That's why we need you to contribute to Mark's Fight Back Fund. It's CRITICAL we hit our one-day goal of $10,000. Will you help us out?](https://act.markwarnerva.com/page/m/7e85d7ef/53d9897e/52353ef8/1c891513/3069277921/VEsC/p/eyJKU1ZOUVVsTVNVNUhTVVFsSlE9PSI6IjMyMCJ9/?g=fQCtPq_HJ6FdDGwS5GeylnA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/warner-1day10k?refcode=20191102nd__320__&refcode2=ask1&amount=5")**

Scott Taylor lost his seat to Elaine Luria after only one term, but during his brief tenure in the House, **he voted with Trump _97.8%_ of the time.**

**When he was a Congressman, Scott Taylor voted for what Trump wanted even when it was wrong for Virginia. Why should we expect Senator Taylor to vote any differently?**

Let's make sure Taylor never again gets the chance to put Donald Trump above Virginians. **Will you donate to Mark 's one-day goal of $10,000?**

name: Bill Bryant
subject: Vote for Ann Davison Sattler
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Forge
body:
Hello neighbor,

I want to remind you to vote and
encourage you to vote for Ann Davison Sattler for Seattle City
Council.

Ann is the pragmatic type of
person we need working for us in Seattle. She's ready to make
tough decisions to solve our homeless crisis; she opposes heroin
injection sites; she believes a council member should respond to
constituents.

We need to make some changes on
the city council. Ann Davison Sattler is part of the change we
need. If you haven't voted yet, please join me in voting for Ann
Davison Sattler, Seattle City Council.

Your neighbor,

name: Jason Yates
subject: The Supreme Court needs to hear from you regarding Roe v. Wade
body:
Sign the petition today!
Hi Peter,
You may have heard that the United States Supreme Court has agreed to hear the controversial Louisiana abortion law case, June Medical Services v. Gee, this term.
The Louisiana state statute would require doctors who perform abortions to have admitting privileges at a nearby hospital. Abortion supporters believe this places an “undue burden” on a woman’s access to abortions, while pro-life advocates think it’s an obvious and necessary health protection.
The Supreme Court reviewed a similar state statute from Texas in 2016, and it deemed unconstitutional. However, with two new justices on the bench, Justice Kavanaugh and Justice Gorsuch, the court has agreed to take the case, and a ruling will be issued right in the midst of the presidential election cycle of 2020.
This case is historic for the pro-life movement. The Supreme Court has an opportunity, right in front of them, to take a big step towards overturning Roe v. Wade.
**Our friends at The Moral Outcry have launched a petition seeking the signatures of 500,000 Americans to be filed in an _Amicus Curiae Brief_ (a friend of the court brief) for the Supreme Court by November 11. The brief will call on the Supreme Court to reverse Roe v. Wade.**
We know this is a bold ask. But we serve a powerful God, and we believe we need to do everything we can to stand for the protection of unborn life.
**So far, The Moral Outcry has gathered over 250,000 signatures, but we need your help to reach the 500,000 goal. Will you please use the link below to sign the petition?**
[https://secured.myfaithvotes.org/the-moral-outcry-petition](https://secured.myfaithvotes.org/the-moral-outcry-petition?e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e191102com01&n=1)
When you sign the petition, your name will be added to the Supreme Court brief filed on behalf of Melina Thybault and the Moral Outcry Petition. I hope I can count on you to sign the brief and pray for this case before the Supreme Court.
**United. We Stand.**
Jason Yates
CEO
[](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=FvZODUdzRCV4_QVEoUI5Y_LYDGbWPUdf00keax2EMv8&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e191102com01&n=2)
©2019 My Faith Votes
10940 S Parker, 640, Parker, CO 80134
This email was sent to [dpwillis67@gmail.com](mailto:dpwillis67@gmail.com).

name: Cory Booker
subject: I see you. I love you.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Cory 2020
body:
| | |
---
| Friend, late last night, I spoke at the Liberty and Justice Celebration in Iowa, and I wanted to share it with you... Ten years ago this month, I was in my third year as mayor of Newark, New Jersey's largest city. We were in the middle of the Great Recession, which for a community that's already struggling is like a depression, and it was hard, but we were pulling together, and we pulled through. But at the same time, I was losing one of the most important people in my life, a guy who was like a second father to me who was in the final stages of his battle with cancer. When I started out working as a tenants' rights lawyer in Newark right out of law school, this guy Frank was one of my first mentors. He had spent his life working as a tenant's rights advocate, leading the longest rent strike in Newark's history. We would hear from families who had to deal with rats, mice, and roaches in rooms where their children slept, people who had to boil water on the stove to keep warm during brutally cold winters. Sometimes, the meetings would go on for hours. I would grow restless and impatient -- most folks were saying much of the same things, and I already had all the information I needed for our case. But Frank never rushed anyone. He sat and he listened. After one especially long meeting, I said something to Frank about how long it was. And without judgement or pride, he told me that the meetings were about more than just fixing the buildings. The meetings were about more than sharing information. They were about making people feel seen and heard and understood. They were about giving people a platform and a place to know that they were not alone. **They were about healing.** Frank got older, and I went on the run for city council, then mayor. By the time I became mayor, Frank's health was failing. Eventually, he went blind. But when I would go visit him or pick him up to go grocery shopping or go out to lunch, I would announce myself, and he would say "I see you Cory." It became our regular greeting. "Hi Frank, it's me, Cory." "I see you." The last time I saw Frank, the nurses at the hospice he was at told me it was nearing the end. He was weaker than I had ever seen him, but when I came in, I announced myself, and he could barely breathe but he got out the words, _" I see you."_ I sat with him, I kissed his forehead. I thanked him. I told him how much he meant to me. I told him I loved him. And before I left, he said to me, _" I love you, too."_ Those were the only two things Frank said to me the last time I saw him. The last two things he said to me. _I see you. I love you._ They were words of empowerment and encouragement and engagement: you are not alone in this. I am here for you, and together we will figure this out. I know there are people who want us to believe that we can't win this election by making it about our values. I believe that in order to heal our country, we must choose unity, we must choose a more courageous empathy, we must choose to recognize that we need each other if we're going to make it. **[I'm asking you to join me because I'm asking you to believe in this campaign, the one that unites itself in common cause to take on the toughest challenges.](https://act.corybooker.%3D/?g=IiwAaFH8x8VcsCn-MvZWseA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/cory2020-em-decdebate?refcode=em_fr_20191102_CB_20191102-fr-CB-LJC-actives_actives_l1&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&city=Cresco&state=PA&zip=18326&phone=&occupation=&employer=")** I know you all have a lot of choices in this primary. But the choice our country will make a little over a year from tonight isn't going to be just about one person or one office. **It won 't be about what the pundits or the polls were saying the year before -- about who was leading or who was polling at roughly 3 percent.** It won't be about who took the best shots against their fellow Democrats on the debate stage. It won't be about who had the biggest fundraising haul or who checked what boxes at what time. I believe that in order to heal our country, we must choose unity, we must choose a more courageous empathy, we must choose to recognize that we need each other if we're going to make it. **I 'm asking you** to believe that we can do more than remove one man from one office, because beating him is the floor, it's not the ceiling. **I 'm asking you** to believe in an America where we come together to take on our biggest challenges -- from the epidemic of gun violence to the disease of addiction. **I 'm asking you** to believe in an America that leads the world because we invest in people. In good jobs in every community. In a high quality public education for every child. **I 'm asking you** to believe in an America that doesn't shrink into isolation in the face of the world's biggest challenges, but leads and unites the world's great democracies to take it on. **I 'm asking you** to believe in an America where you can turn on the news and see our leaders and feel pride and not shame. **I 'm asking you** to believe in an America where we revive civic grace, where we extend a more courageous empathy to one another and where nobody gets left on the sidelines and nobody gets left behind. **I 'm asking you to believe that you are not alone in this.** Together we will channel our common pain, defeat Donald Trump, and we will win back the White House. **As your president, we will heal our country.** And together, we will rise. Thank you,
Cory [](https://act.corybooker.%3D/?g=IiwAaFH8x8VcsCn-MvZWseA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/cory2020-em-decdebate?refcode=em_fr_20191102_CB_20191102-fr-CB-LJC-actives_actives_contribbutton&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&city=Cresco&state=PA&zip=18326&phone=&occupation=&employer=")

clean_body: | | | | Friend, late last night, I spoke at the Liberty and Justice Celebration in Iowa, and I wanted to share it with you... Ten years ago this month, I was in my third year as mayor of Newark, New Jersey's largest city. We were in the middle of the Great Recession, which for a community that's already struggling is like a depression, and it was hard, but we were pulling together, and we pulled through. But at the same time, I was losing one of the most important people in my life, a guy who was like a second father to me who was in the final stages of his battle with cancer. When I started out working as a tenants' rights lawyer in Newark right out of law school, this guy Frank was one of my first mentors. He had spent his life working as a tenant's rights advocate, leading the longest rent strike in Newark's history. We would hear from families who had to deal with rats, mice, and roaches in rooms where their children slept, people who had to boil water on the stove to keep warm during brutally cold winters. Sometimes, the meetings would go on for hours. I would grow restless and impatient -- most folks were saying much of the same things, and I already had all the information I needed for our case. But Frank never rushed anyone. He sat and he listened. After one especially long meeting, I said something to Frank about how long it was. And without judgement or pride, he told me that the meetings were about more than just fixing the buildings. The meetings were about more than sharing information. They were about making people feel seen and heard and understood. They were about giving people a platform and a place to know that they were not alone. They were about healing. Frank got older, and I went on the run for city council, then mayor. By the time I became mayor, Frank's health was failing. Eventually, he went blind. But when I would go visit him or pick him up to go grocery shopping or go out to lunch, I would announce myself, and he would say "I see you Cory." It became our regular greeting. "Hi Frank, it's me, Cory." "I see you." The last time I saw Frank, the nurses at the hospice he was at told me it was nearing the end. He was weaker than I had ever seen him, but when I came in, I announced myself, and he could barely breathe but he got out the words, " I see you." I sat with him, I kissed his forehead. I thanked him. I told him how much he meant to me. I told him I loved him. And before I left, he said to me, " I love you, too." Our democracy has been hijacked by the rich and the powerful.

They made it work for themselves, and they have left everyone else behind. And we see the effects each and every day:
* We see it when corporate executives cut pay and lay off workers.
* We see it when insurance companies deny patients treatments they need and access to doctors they count on.
* We see it when our broken criminal justice system destroys Black and Brown families.

The list goes on: A generation crushed under the weight of student debt. The gun industry blocks reforms that would save lives. Big oil companies destroy our planet and keep calling the shots in Washington.

That's corruption, pure and simple -- and it's time to call it out and fight back. If we as a country want to meet the challenges of this moment in history, we must fight for big, structural change.

**[The rich and powerful will do whatever it takes to stop us-- so it's going to take all of us to win this fight. >
Some are afraid because they're part of the wealthy and well-connected and they could lose their influence.

Some are afraid because they have built their own success around sucking up to the wealthy and well-connected.

And some are afraid because they've become so cynical or beaten down that they just don't think real change is possible.

Not me. I'm not afraid -- I'm in this fight all the way.

Even if the rich and powerful don't like it, even if the fat cats from Big Ag and Big Tech and Big Pharma say no, we need big ideas so we can actually fix the challenges of our time.

We need big ideas to inspire people -- to come out and caucus, and to come out and vote.

We need big ideas to take back the U.S. Senate in 2020 to put Mitch McConnell out of a job.

We need big ideas and, the absolutely critical part, we need to be willing to fight for them.

This is a time of crisis. Washington insiders, media pundits, fancy experts -- even plenty of people in our own party -- want to deny it. They think running some vague campaign that nibbles around the edges is the safe strategy.

But if the best we can do is to promise to return to "business as usual" after Donald Trump, _Democrats will lose._

**We win elections when we have solutions that are big enough to meet the challenges that people face in their lives. Because fear and complacency don't win elections; hope and courage win elections.**

**[It's time for big ideas. Chip in now and let's get this done.](https://my.elizabethwarren.com/page/m/28528d2d/45c30650/106e793a6/66f57e19/366500660/VEsHAg/p/eyJKU1ZsYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsImUzc2dkWEpzSUgxOSI6ImV3LW5vdjE5ZnItc3RpY2tlciIsImUzc2djbVZtWTI5a1pTQjlmUT09IjoiRU1MMTVGX0VHUl9CU0NfRV8yMDE5MTEwMl9WMVMzX1AifQ%3D%3D/?g=4qwscq_HJ6FcsC0VbCsFfMA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ew-nov19fr-sticker?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&refcode=EML15F_EGR_BSC_E_20191102_V1S3_P")**

Thanks for being a part of this,

Elizabeth

clean_body: The rich and powerful will do whatever it takes to stop us -- so it's going to take all of us to win this fight. | | | "" Our democracy has been hijacked by the rich and the powerful. They made it work for themselves, and they have left everyone else behind. And we see the effects each and every day: We see it when corporate executives cut pay and lay off workers. We see it when insurance companies deny patients treatments they need and access to doctors they count on. We see it when our broken criminal justice system destroys Black and Brown families. The list goes on: A generation crushed under the weight of student debt. The gun industry blocks reforms that would save lives. Big oil companies destroy our planet and keep calling the shots in Washington. That's corruption, pure and simple -- and it's time to call it out and fight back. If we as a country want to meet the challenges of this moment in history, we must fight for big, structural change. "" | If you've saved payment info with ActBlue Express, your donation will process automatically: | | | | "" | "" | | "" | "" | | "" | "" Some are afraid because they're part of the wealthy and well-connected and they could lose their influence. Some are afraid because they have built their own success around sucking up to the wealthy and well-connected. And some are afraid because they've become so cynical or beaten down that they just don't think real change is possible. Not me. I'm not afraid -- I'm in this fight all the way. Even if the rich and powerful don't like it, even if the fat cats from Big Ag and Big Tech and Big Pharma say no, we need big ideas so we can actually fix the challenges of our time. We need big ideas to inspire people -- to come out and caucus, and to come out and vote. We need big ideas to take back the U.S. Senate in 2020 to put Mitch McConnell out of a job. We need big ideas and, the absolutely critical part, we need to be willing to fight for them. This is a time of crisis. Washington insiders, media pundits, fancy experts -- even plenty of people in our own party -- want to deny it. They think running some vague campaign that nibbles around the edges is the safe strategy. But if the best we can do is to promise to return to "business as usual" after Donald Trump, Democrats will lose. We win elections when we have solutions that are big enough to meet the challenges that people face in their lives. Because fear and complacency don't win elections; hope and courage win elections. ""** Thanks for being a part of this, Elizabeth "" | | | "" | "" | "" | "" | "" | "" ---|---|---|---|---|--- | Paid for by Warren for President All content (C) 2019 Warren for President, All Rights Reserved PO Box 171375, Boston, MA 02117

name: Senator Tina Smith
subject: Will you co-sponsor this with me, friend?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Tina Smith for Minnesota
body:
Wind power is the way of the future. That's why I've introduced a bill to continue to increase investment in wind energy. Will you be a citizen co-sponsor for my bill?

| [](https://action.tinaforminnesota.%3D/?g=YCjTcDpTVzRarKyUt8_RZpA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/tsm-email-nov2019?refcode=em191102windpower-full&recurring=auto&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&firstname=&lastname=&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=PA&phone=&employer=&occupation=") Team,

I've said it before and I'll say it again: Tackling our climate crisis requires serious, quick, and bold action.

That's why I'm writing to you today -- I've just introduced a bipartisan bill to increase investments in wind energy, so Minnesota can keep leading the U.S. in this industry and so we can set our country back on course to be a leader in addressing climate change.

This bill - the Wind Energy Research and Development Act - will expand the resources available for wind energy development and make the adoption of this clean energy source more accessible. This is the action we need to address climate change.

**If we're going to prioritize action on climate change, we've got to work together.**

**[That's why I'm asking you to be a citizen co-sponsor of the Wind Energy Research and Development Act. Adding your name sends a clear message that it's time to act now to protect our environment, slow climate change, and expand wind energy in Minnesota and across the country.](https://action.tinaforminnesota.%3D/?g=YCjTcDpTVzRarKyUt8_RZpA "https://action.tinaforminnesota.com/page/s/windenergy?refcode=em191102windpower-full&recurring=auto&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&firstname=&lastname=&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=PA&phone=&employer=&occupation=")**
[](https://action.tinaforminnesota.%3D/?g=YCjTcDpTVzRarKyUt8_RZpA "https://action.tinaforminnesota.com/page/s/windenergy?refcode=em191102windpower-full&recurring=auto&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&firstname=&lastname=&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=PA&phone=&employer=&occupation=") I'm proud that Minnesota is already leading the way. Right now, Minnesota gets nearly 20% of the state's energy from wind power -- that's over a million homes powered by clean energy. It's time for the rest of the country to follow our lead.

But this fight isn't easy -- it's why we must work together to save our planet from irreversible damage. With continual investments in clean energy, we can keep growing the industry and expanding economic opportunities in rural communities -- all positioning the United States to be a leader in tackling the climate crisis.

**[This is about public health, our environment, and making sure America is leading when it comes to tackling the climate crisis -- and we can't do this without you.](https://action.tinaforminnesota.%3D/?g=YCjTcDpTVzRarKyUt8_RZpA "https://action.tinaforminnesota.com/page/s/windenergy?refcode=em191102windpower-full&recurring=auto&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&firstname=&lastname=&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=PA&phone=&employer=&occupation=")**

Thank you for your support,

Tina | **U.S. Senator Tina Smith is running for re-election in 2020 in a race _NBC News_ has already labeled a "race to watch" with Donald Trump targeting Minnesota for victory. Make sure she has the resources to fight back.** [CONTRIBUTE](https://action.tinaforminnesota.%3D%3D/?g=YCjTcDpTVzRarKyUt8_RZpA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/tsm-email-nov2019?refcode=em191102windpower-full-biobox&amount=10&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&firstname=&lastname=&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=PA&phone=&employer=&occupation=&recurring=auto")

body:
Share your story.

| [](http://act.yang2020.com/go/68?t=1&akid=2494%2E377263%2ET3W8Gh) Friend --

Andrew has a vision for this country -- one where all Americans can prosper. He has solutions to the problems this country is facing and he has created a community that believes the same -- the Yang Gang.

**We noticed that you haven’t yet donated to the campaign (if we’re wrong about this, we apologize -- sometimes donations come from other emails you use and we can’t always keep track of that) and we’re curious what’s holding you back.**

We know we send a lot of fundraising emails, but to build a strong campaign to win, we need grassroots support.

Every dollar we receive through emails like this goes towards critical investments: opening field offices, hiring organizers, and investing in ad buys to persuade voters before the primaries.

**Since this support is so critical, we wanted to come to you and ask how we can do better. What’s holding you back from donating to our campaign?**

Friends - our campaign is rooted in the issues that matter to you.

name: Mike Franken
subject: Thank you
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Franken for Iowa
body:
A campaign asks a lot of all involved, especially its supporters. Last month we tasked you to help us hit our fundraising goal, and I'm happy to report that your efforts paid off -- we made our goal.
|
---

| | Friends -- A campaign asks a lot of all involved, especially its supporters. Last month we tasked you to help us hit our fundraising goal, and I'm happy to report that your efforts paid off -- we made our goal. This is only the second full month of this movement to remove Joni Ernst from office, but we're off to a tremendous start. I am working to win this race because I know what Iowans want from our government:
* Access to quality, affordable health care
* Policy that supports our farmers by safeguarding their land against climate change, and ensuring that their livelihood isn't sacrificed to bad trade deals and corporate greed
* A body of lawmakers that understand their awesome responsibility, and strive to live up to a higher standard of decorum

In the weeks and months to come, you're going to hear a lot from me about my proposals for our great state, for our nation, and for our international relations. Why such a broad topic area? Because Iowans want developed, insightful, and executable proposals which unite our country and make it stronger, not empty platitudes that divide us and others. Thank you for your support. I'm excited to have you on board this principles-first campaign. Very respectfully,
Mike
---
|
---

| | Paid for by Franken for Iowa
---

subject: Trump thinks impeachment is "The Greatest Witch Hunt in American History!"
disclaimer_text: Paid for by When Democrats Turn Out PAC
body:
|

| | Peter, In an on-brand Halloween Twitter rant, Trump called the impeachment inquiry "The Greatest Witch Hunt in American History!" and said that the "Impeachment Hoax is hurting our Stock Market." But the only thing that's hurting is his approval numbers \-- 54% of Americans disapprove of him. | | Derek,

I've spent a lot of time on the road in Virginia lately campaigning for our General Assembly candidates, and one thing is clear to me everywhere I go: _Virginians know our upcoming elections are special._

They also know a few other things:
* Virginians know that America is watching the Commonwealth, and what _we_ do in November could lay the groundwork for _huge_ Democratic victories in 2020.
* They know that we have the opportunity to elect a blue General Assembly in Virginia for the _first_ time in over twenty years.
* And most of all, they know that we're ready to see _change_ happen across Virginia.

That's why I've been out there working to make sure we flip the General Assembly blue this November and then keep Virginia blue in 2020. **And it's why I'm partnering with the Virginia House and Senate Caucuses today.**

**If you 're ready to make change happen in 2019 and 2020, I want to personally ask you to contribute to this joint effort today.
